TextENGNew: Please note.@rThe opening animation that follows@rcontains many spoilers for the main story.@rAlso, since Hirukowashi-hen is based on@r"Higurashi Daybreak," some content@rmay differ from the main story.@rPlease keep this in mind, nipah☆
changeReason: The old text is an entirely rewritten meta-warning about skipping the chapter, not a translation of the Japanese; the new translation faithfully renders the original notice about the opening animation containing spoilers and its Higurashi Daybreak origins.

TextENGNew: ``Roo-roon, run-ta-ta, run-ta-ta~♪``
``Judging by how cheerful you are, there must be a new pile of trash at the dam site.``

``Yep, that's right~! I wonder what's buried in the new pile! I have a feeling there's something adorable waiting for me~! As soon as I get home I'm heading straight there for the treasure hunt! Keiichi-kun, won't you come with me? Please?``
``Me?  Hmm... I still haven't finished reading all the manga Mion lent me.``
``Uu~ A girl invites you out and you'd rather read manga... Hau. ...And to think I went out of my way to help you during club today. So this is my reward...``
``N-No, I'm sorry, I lied! Three cheers for treasure hunting! Please, please allow me to accompany you on your expedition, Rena-san!!``
...Club activities could be summed up in one word: ruthless. Every man for himself — and when someone showed weakness, you crushed them completely and sealed their last-place fate.
When I had the upper hand I would stomp all over the others, and when the tables were turned, I got stomped in return.
But today luck had simply abandoned me, and every big gamble I made blew up in my face.
changeReason: The old translation refers to 'the deciding match' (singular), but the Japanese ことごとく大勝負が裏目に出た means 'every big gamble backfired,' indicating multiple failures rather than one.

TextENGNew: ...And that was when Rena had come to my rescue.
``But you ended up in last place anyway. Ahahahaha, the punishment game this Sunday is going to be so much fun! So much fun!``
``......Uuuu, after all the help you gave me, this is how it ends... I'm hopeless...``
In the end, I couldn't turn Rena's help into anything, and last place was sealed.
...But there was no doubt that it was thanks to Rena that I'd at least been able to challenge that final all-or-nothing match.
If she hadn't helped me, I would have lost so badly that I wouldn't have even had the chance to make that last stand.
Rena had no obligation to rescue me from last place. Normally, once you knew you couldn't hope for first, you'd think at the very least about avoiding last.
Leaving me alone would have locked in my last place. There was nothing to be gained from saving me — only risk.
And yet Rena had helped me anyway.
...That single shard of kindness in the merciless world of our club was sublime — like the light of the Buddha reaching down into hell.
Fighting without mercy was the one rule of our club.
And yet, even in that world, Rena was the one who never lost her kindness — who never stopped wanting everyone to enjoy themselves playing games together.
``Ahahahaha, you're giving me too much credit. ...Rena just hates one-sided games. I mean, anyone would get sulky if they knew no comeback was possible no matter how hard they fought.``

``W-Well, I suppose. ...I mean, once it was decided I'd lose, they started taking my measurements for the punishment game before it was even over... Ugh, what on earth are those measurements for... Woe is me...``

``I believe you can always turn things around, no matter how bad it gets. ...No, more than that — the world has to be that kind of place. That's why Rena just can't help giving a chance to someone who's truly in trouble.``
``......Rena, you really are kind. ...Hearing that, I feel a pang of conscience thinking about how mercilessly I knocked you down when you were struggling... Uuu!``
``Ahahaha, Rena does it because she wants to. I'm not asking you to do the same. But, well... when Rena is in a pinch, it would make her really happy if you repaid the favor! Really!``
``Sure! Today's counts as a debt I owe you! Well, setting that aside — I'll come along on your treasure hunts once in a while. So, we're meeting at the trash pile at the dam site, right?``
``Yep! Let's meet at the treasure mountain at the dam site!!``
Rena answered with the brightest smile, then ran off waving her arm...
